<!-- Auto-generated multi-agent peer review: 7 expert reviewer lenses -> adversarial grounding verification (every finding re-checked against the manuscript text) -> senior-editor synthesis. -->

## 1. Summary of the paper

The manuscript presents a single gradient-free recipe — CMA-ES optimizing compact policy networks (predominantly oblique soft decision trees) with problem-specific *action decoders* — applied across ten classical inventory-control families (lost sales, dual sourcing, multi-echelon serial/divergent, perishable, one-warehouse multi-retailer, ameliorating inventory, general-network backorder, and a production/assembly/distribution network). Its central conceptual claim is that the *action parameterization/decoder geometry is itself part of the policy to be learned*, not a fixed property of the environment, and that this — rather than learner capacity or depth — is where the leverage lives. The paper is distinguished by an unusually disciplined "match / beat / gap-to-bound" verdict vocabulary, an explicit five-seed reporting standard, careful separation of like-for-like (same-protocol tuned heuristic) beats from cross-protocol DRL (A3C/PPO) "context," and a validation appendix that re-runs exact solvers and simulations against published numbers.

## 2. Overall recommendation

**Major revision.** This is a broad, careful, and genuinely honest empirical paper whose core idea is real and well-supported on most of its hardest rows (OWMR heterogeneous instances, general-network, mixed-assembly are all properly seed-robust), so rejection would be wrong. However, two convergent rigor failures block acceptance as written: (a) the paper's *first and most-advertised* contribution — the 22/24 and 47/48 lost-sales win counts — rests on single-optimizer-seed tables that violate the paper's own five-seed standard (flagged independently by four reviewers), and (b) the abstract calls single-/two-seed PADN serial/assembly beats "robust," directly contradicting the paper's own table footnote. These, plus uncited load-bearing positioning/method claims and the absence of any compute reporting behind a "more sample-efficient than DRL" claim, are fixable but material. None touch a *central* numerical result, which is why this is a revision and not a reject.

## 3. Strengths

- **Conceptual delta is crisp and falsifiable.** "The action parameterization is part of the policy, not a fixed property of the environment" (Sec 3.1, lines 294–312; contribution 2, lines 220–226) is genuinely distinct from generic ES-for-control and is shown to be load-bearing (multi-echelon direct-level vs reduced-grid; dual-sourcing CDI coordinates).
- **Honest, disciplined verdict accounting.** The three-verdict vocabulary (beat / match / gap-to-bound) is defined and applied with real rigor; sub-band margins are downgraded to "matches," not wins (dual sourcing −0.009%/−0.041%, lines 1370–1373; serial below-optimum costs, lines 2797–2806).
- **Exemplary comparator fairness.** A3C/PPO are repeatedly and explicitly labeled cross-protocol context rather than head-to-head beats (OWMR lines 3062–3070, 3117–3120; dual sourcing 1380–1382; multi-echelon 1738–1744), pre-empting the most common inventory-RL objection.
- **Genuine seed-robustness on the most contested rows.** General-network set-1 (24.3%±1.8%, 5 per-seed costs, line 2466), OWMR (4.61/7.07/12.44%, line 3110–3112), and the mixed production-assembly residual-head row (−2.20%, 5 per-seed values, line 3666) are reported with per-seed values and all-seeds-below statements.
- **Validation appendix is overwhelmingly EXECUTED, not snapshotted.** Fixed-cost lost sales (Table 13/22), perishable (Table 16/25), and serial Clark-Scarf (Table 18/28) reproduce the published optimum via independent exact solvers (average-cost VI, exact tabular MDP, recursive newsvendor) AND the environment simulation, with explicit gaps. This is the correct standard and is rare in this literature.
- **OR formulations are correct and subtle.** Lost-sales vs backorder vs partial-backorder dynamics, order-after-demand timing yielding the L_k (not L_k+1) window (lines 2654–2656), echelon-vs-installation accounting, and the observation that the textbook 47.65 echelon optimum is structurally unreachable by the network's local pairwise policy (lines 3599–3604) are all handled correctly.
- **Reproducibility scaffolding.** Disjoint search/validation/evaluation seed blocks with candid leakage disclosure (selecting perishable FIFO on the eval block flips it to a −0.49% loss, lines 1971–1972); worked-example traces and two-implementation rollout cost-match validation (lines 910–916, 1326–1329).
- **Strong presentation craft.** All 15 figures render legibly; custom TikZ environment-flow diagrams are a distinctive contribution; most headline tables carry units, ± dispersion, "lower/higher is better" guidance, bolded best, and footnotes distinguishing like-for-like from cross-protocol. Parallel ten-problem section structure makes a long paper navigable; no internal jargon leaks into the manuscript.
- **All 31 bibliography entries are cited; no orphan references.** Heuristic literatures (Zipkin, Xin, Bijvank, Veeraraghavan, Sheopuri) are correctly attributed.

## 4. Major issues

These must be fixed before acceptance. Several were raised independently by multiple reviewer lenses; convergence is noted because it strengthens the case.

### M1. The headline lost-sales win counts (22/24, 47/48) rest on single-seed tables that violate the paper's own five-seed standard. *(Convergence: raised under Experimental Rigor W1 [critical], Technical Soundness W2, Claims Audit context, and Figures/Tables V1 — four independent lenses.)*
The Section 3.3 protocol (lines 501–504) pledges "mean ± standard deviation across five independent CMA-ES optimizer seeds … in place of any single-seed or best-of-N figure." But Tables 2–3 (lines 993–1110), which underpin contribution 1 (abstract lines 121–122; contribution lines 206–207), report one bare cost per cell with no ±, and the per-cell dagger-exclusion mechanism (lines 480–490) only makes sense if each cell is a *single* optimizer run. With many learned-vs-heuristic margins <1% (e.g. 4.749 vs 4.750) and daggered divergences present, a per-instance win/loss verdict is not robust to optimizer initialization — exactly the property the paper insists on elsewhere.
**Fix (strongly prefer (a)):** (a) re-run each of the 72 cells over ≥5 optimizer seeds and report mean (with std, or at least the all-seeds-best fraction), re-deriving 22/24 and 47/48 under that convention; or (b) state explicitly in Section 5 *and* the abstract that the lost-sales surface is single-optimizer-seed (evaluation-seed-averaged only), exempt it under the "unless stated otherwise" clause, soften "instance-best in 22/24" to a single-run observation, and specify how diverged cells are counted in the win totals (loss vs excluded — currently a selection effect, Rigor W6).

### M2. The abstract calls single-/two-seed PADN beats "robust," contradicting the table's own footnote. *(Convergence: Technical Soundness W1 [major], Rigor W3 [major], OR W9 [minor].)*
The abstract (lines 139–142) says the policy is "robustly beating the environment's own pairwise base-stock on the serial, pure-assembly, and … mixed topologies." But the Table 17 footnote (line 3709) states the serial case-3 and pure-assembly rows are single-/two-seed with a multi-seed audit *pending*; only the mixed topology has the five-seed audit. The serial run1/run2 spread is −4.96%/−8.77% — a 3.8-point swing between two seeds — precisely the regime where a "beat" can be a seed artifact (cf. the ±22.89 between-seed scatter this family shows, line 3661). The introduction (lines 215–218) already words this correctly.
**Fix:** Reword the abstract to match the intro and footnote — reserve "robust/seed-robust" for the mixed topology (−2.20%, five-seed); describe serial and pure-assembly as single-/two-seed beats pending audit, propagating the caveat to the contributions list (lines 216–217) and discussion (line 3794). Better still, run the pending audit before claiming those two beats.

### M3. Internal seed-count contradiction (6 vs 5) on the multi-echelon headline. *(Convergence: Technical Soundness W4, Rigor W4, Claims Audit W1 — three lenses, all flag it.)*
The setup (lines 1720–1721) says "horizon 3×10⁴, 6 seeds," but every reported multi-echelon statistic (14.7%±1.6%, "all 5 seeds below," footnote line 1764) is over five seeds. A reader cannot tell whether a sixth run was dropped (a silent best-of-N exclusion) or "6" is a typo — and this underpins a headline 14.7%/12.0% beat.
**Fix:** Reconcile to one number. If six were run and one diverged/was excluded, say so with the dagger convention; otherwise correct line 1721 to "5." State the exact N for every reported std, and disambiguate optimizer seeds vs evaluation (demand-path) seeds as the perishable section does.

### M4. Best-of-N cost reporting in flagship tables. *(Rigor W2 [major].)*
While dual-sourcing, perishable, and production sections report a five-seed mean±std on Δ%, the absolute cost numbers in the tables and prose are best-run/headline-seed values, contradicting the line-504 pledge. Perishable Table 8 cost cells are explicitly "the headline seed" (line 2058); production Table 17 bolds two *different* single-seed runs (−4.96% and −8.77%, lines 3692–3694) as if both are results; dual-sourcing Table 6 reports no optimizer-seed count or dispersion on its cost cells at all (only 70 CRN evaluation seeds, line 1342) yet prose claims "seed-robustly" (line 1392).
**Fix:** Make the five-seed mean cost (with ±) the primary table value in every learned row; relabel any single-run number as "best/illustrative seed" in a footnote only. Do not bold two single-seed runs as parallel results. Add the optimizer-seed count and dispersion to dual-sourcing cost cells. *(Note: the multi-echelon table row at line 1759 IS already a seed-mean±std — the "best run" phrasing at line 1651 refers to the worked example, not the headline.)*

### M5. Two load-bearing claims carry zero citations. *(Novelty W1 [major] + W2 [major].)*
(a) The central positioning statement that "evolutionary methods have chiefly been used to tune the parameters of fixed-form heuristics rather than to learn generic policies" appears verbatim twice (lines 267–270 and 3741–3743) with no citation — an uncited "no one has done X" against a large GA/ES-for-(s,S)/base-stock OR literature reads as unsurveyed and makes the gap unfalsifiable. (b) The oblique soft decision tree with sigmoid routing and path-probability mixing (lines 360–372) — the dominant backbone in 7 of 10 problems — is presented de novo with no attribution; grep confirms no Frosst/Irsoy/Suárez/Lutsko anywhere, despite a clear lineage (Suárez & Lutsko 1999; Irsoy, Yıldız & Alpaydın 2012; Frosst & Hinton 2017).
**Fix:** (a) Cite 2–4 prior GA/ES heuristic-tuning works you contrast against, then sharpen the delta to "learning the decoder/action geometry, not just heuristic parameters." (b) Add a one-sentence soft-tree attribution at line 360–362 and state that your contribution is the inventory-specific leaf decoders, not the routing mechanism.

### M6. No compute/wall-clock reporting behind the "more sample-efficient than DRL" claim. *(Rigor W5 [major].)*
Line 3851 claims CMA-ES is "far more sample-efficient than A3C here," and the cost-complexity frontier argument (lines 3734–3737) is purely qualitative; yet no section reports CPU/GPU hours, wall-clock, or total environment-steps, and generation counts are given for some problems (OWMR, ameliorating, production) but omitted for lost sales, dual sourcing, multi-echelon, and serial — blocking reproduction.
**Fix:** Add a compute table (per problem: population, generations, total simulated periods, wall-clock, hardware, and the comparator's training budget where available). Replace "far more sample-efficient than A3C" with the measured period/wall-clock ratio or delete the quantitative qualifier. Fill in the four missing generation counts.

### M7. Related work omits the two most relevant prior works it describes. *(Novelty W3 [major].)*
The canonical beer-game DRL reference (Oroojlooyjadid, Nazari, Snyder & Takáč, *A deep Q-network for the beer game*, M&SOM 2022) is *described* at line 254–255 but left uncited; the citation there attaches only to the A3C clause. And the differentiable-simulation / pathwise-gradient inventory line (e.g. Madeka, Torkkola et al., *Deep Inventory Management*, 2022) — the single most natural gradient-based competitor to a gradient-free recipe — is absent, despite the paper making the non-smooth/clipped-action argument for going gradient-free at lines 178–181.
**Fix:** Cite Oroojlooyjadid et al. (2022) at line 255; add 1–2 sentences positioning against differentiable-simulation learning and connect it explicitly to the non-smooth-action argument. Expand the three-paragraph related-work section accordingly.

## 5. Minor issues & clarity

1. **OWMR directional contradiction (cost-min wording).** (Technical W3.) Lines 3140–3143 say PPO "is *cheaper* than both the learned policy and the best heuristic" yet "the learned policy stays *below* PPO here" — in a cost-min setting "below" reads as better, contradicting "cheaper." The −1.05%/−1.96%/−6.43% signs are also inverted vs the negative-is-better Δ% convention (learned is actually +1.05% costlier). Fix to "remains above PPO in cost (PPO is cheaper)" and align signs.
2. **Dual-sourcing l_r=3,4 "optimal proxy" is asserted, not validated.** (OR2.) The bounded-DP for l_r=3,4 is admitted "mildly inconsistent" (falls below the heuristic cost, lines 4177–4180) yet its reproduced gaps are tabled at the same "≤0.01 pp" fidelity as the validated l_r=2 rows (lines 4211–4223). Either run a convergence-in-the-box study analogous to l_r=2, or relabel the (3,*),(4,*) cells "not independently validated (bounded-DP truncated)" and rest the CDI-proxy claim on the validated l_r=2 cells plus the regime sweep.
3. **Figure/equation quantity mismatches.** (Technical W6, OR3.) Perishable figure node charges holding on start-of-period youngest bucket o¹ (line 1815) while Eq 10 charges post-issue survivors r^j (line 1902); OWMR figure charges h_w(y^w)⁺ on inventory position (line 2848) while Eq 16 charges h_w I^w on on-hand (line 2928); dual-sourcing figure attaches c_r to the arriving order (line 1157) while Eq 5 charges it on the placed order (line 1262, expectation-equivalent). Serial figure labels echelon symbol h^k on installation on-hand (lines 2589–2591, caption 2615) while the body reserves h^k for echelon *differences* and the cost eq uses installation H^k (lines 2632–2633, 2692). Make figure labels use the same symbols/quantities as the governing equations; in a Clark-Scarf section the echelon/installation distinction is load-bearing.
4. **CMA-ES mean-update subscript.** (Technical W5.) The display equation (line 423) writes samples as θ^{n'}_{i+1} in the update producing μ_{i+1}, but they are drawn from N(μ_i,·); Algorithm 1 (line 465) correctly uses θ^{(n)}_i. Change to θ^{n'}_i.
5. **Heuristic comparators partly literature-quoted vs re-tuned.** (Rigor W7.) Lost-sales heuristics use "literature-reported parameters where available" (lines 974–975); multi-echelon NDP/A3C costs are published numbers (1713–1715). State explicitly whether the lost-sales myopic/SVBS/(s,S)/(s,nQ)/(s,S,q) parameters are re-optimized under the paper's own estimator (as perishable/OWMR/general-network are), so a "beat" is not partly an artifact of a transcribed parameter.
6. **Per-instance architecture/decoder selection undisclosed.** (Rigor W9 + Writing W6.) OWMR rows report the better of depth-2/depth-3 per instance (Table 16, lines 3104–3106) and the heterogeneous-10 constant-leaf geometry was "found by a search over the decoder classes" (lines 3046–3050) — but the size/automation/budget of that search is never defined (the phrase recurs ~6×: abstract 143, intro 219, OWMR 2973–2976, mixed-network 3663). Given this "search" turns a tie into +12.44% and a +2.82% loss into a −2.20% beat, define it: candidate set, scoring, count, manual vs automated, held-out vs evaluation selection. State whether the runner-up architecture also beats the heuristic.
7. **Perishable corroboration instances lack seed std.** (Rigor W10.) Table 9 (lines 2091–2099) reports only paired evaluation SEM, not the five-optimizer-seed std used for the anchors (Table 8, line 2046), yet is presented to show the beat generalizes. Add the five-seed std or relabel as single-seed corroboration and soften "again beats."
8. **Population-std vs sample-std inconsistency.** (Claims Audit W2.) The mixed-network row reports ±2.49 (n-divisor population std) while GBK (±189.7) and divergent-KT (±11.3) use n−1 sample std; the per-seed values give sample std 2.78. Use the sample (n−1) convention everywhere → 291.13±2.78, and the footnote's mean+std becomes 293.92. (Conclusion survives either way.)
9. **Reproducibility artifact unlocatable.** (Rigor W8.) The "open-source repository" (lines 511–515) carries no URL/DOI/commit, and the "baseline ledger" is framed as future work (lines 3809–3812). Add a concrete (anonymized-for-review) link + commit hash, and ensure per-problem CMA-ES config (population, generations, σ, κ, seed lists) and demand-generator seeds for every table are in the appendix or repo.
10. **Two cited demand processes silently recalibrated.** (OR overall assessment, fidelity.) Van Roy case study 2 uses latent mean 1.0 in place of the stated μ=0, and the "simple problem" uses mean 6.294 for N(5,8²) — so the multi-echelon environment is not the cited environment on its own demand input. Flag these recalibrations prominently where the headline comparison is made.
11. **Validation-appendix descriptor slips.** (OR4, OR5, OR8.) Vanilla lost-sales "optimal 4.73" is a stored catalog literal, not a re-derivation (line 4090) — re-run the VI solver (capability exists in the fixed-cost section) or label "catalog (not re-derived)." The perishable "critical-period-7 slice" descriptor (line 4232) is undefined/garbled — replace with the actual m=2, L=1 parameters. "Certifies the environment's dynamics" (line 4353) should be scoped to the *single-node* reduction; multi-node transitions are verified only equation-by-equation.
12. **"Instance-best" / "previously-unsolved" / "published benchmark" wording precision.** (Claims Audit W8, W4, W9; Novelty W4.) "Instance-best in 22/24" means best among reported comparators, not optimal — in the four cells with a known optimum the learned policy is 0.1–0.4% above it (lines 1033–1036); add a one-line clarifier. "Previously-unsolved" for OWMR heterogeneous-10 (abstract line 136) actually means the authors' *own* prior tie, on a row where published PPO (42835.02) already beats the learned policy (44170.26, line 3106) — rescope to "an instance our earlier search could only tie." GBK "beats the published benchmark by over 20%" (abstract line 131) is measured against the *reproduced* benchmark (10354.8); say "(reproduced) … under paired CRN." Mirror the honest limitations precision into the abstract/contribution-1 breadth claim (which is externally validated on ~7–8 of 10 families).

## 6. Academic writing / copyedit notes

1. **Abstract 123-word run-on (lines 133–142).** (Writing W1 [major].) A single sentence packs three problem verdicts, two PPO caveats, and a method aside. Split into three short sentences, one per problem family, each stating the verdict and dropping inline percentages (move exact numbers to the body).
2. **Contribution bullet 1 is a 314-word paragraph (lines 194–219).** (Writing W2 [major].) It re-lists all ten problems with per-instance percentages that recur verbatim in the abstract, defeating the scannable purpose of an itemized list. Cut to 2–3 sentences asserting the claim type; let the sections prove it.
3. **Consolidate the repeated caveats — the single largest source of bloat.** (Writing W3, W4, W7, W10.) The cross-protocol / like-for-like / research-result-not-a-published-beat conventions, the "no fixed ordering cost" fact, the PPO non-beat, and the "action-geometry-is-the-lever" thesis are each restated in near-identical wording across body + figure caption + table footnote in nearly every networked section (e.g. cross-protocol at 2436–2438, 2519–2520, 3669–3674; "no fixed cost" at body 2434–2435 and caption 2213–2214; action-geometry at 1382, 2432–2433, 3639–3640 before the 3745–3764 synthesis). State each convention ONCE in Section 3 (a "How to read the result tables" paragraph), then reference it in a single clause per section. Reserve the action-geometry synthesis for the Discussion and forward-reference it elsewhere. This alone could cut several pages.
4. **Figure-caption / in-figure / body triplication of the per-period event sequence (≈11 figures).** (Writing W8.) Each environment figure draws a boxed event-sequence panel, re-narrates it in caption prose (circularly pointing to "the boxed panel beneath"), and again in a "Timing of a period" body paragraph (e.g. lines 567–573, 1480–1492, 1835–1845). Let the in-figure panel carry the sequence; shorten captions to one sentence (environment name, source, cost labels); keep the full narration in the body only.
5. **Duplicated word "independent independent" in the Table 1 caption (lines 881–882).** (Writing W5, Figures V5.) Delete one "independent." The phrase "independent policy forward by construction" also recurs at 905–906, 915–916 — vary or consolidate.
6. **Tone: reduce defensive over-hedging.** (Writing W7 [nit].) Phrases like "the honest headline is narrower than a best-seed story" (line 3056) and triple-stated PPO non-beats (interpretation 3062–3067, results 3117+, limitations 3839–3845) tip from rigor into a defensive tone that dilutes genuine wins. State each non-beat once (limitations); make the positive claim cleanly in-section.
7. **Notation polish.** (Writing W9, Technical W7.) κ is rebound per problem (=20 line 727, =100 line 1654, etc.) with no consolidated table — add one line in Section 3 and tabulate per-problem κ. Rename the MMPP regime variable S_t∈{low,high} (line 956) to avoid colliding with the bold state vector **S**_t (line 644). Standardize population/elite symbols across Algorithm 1 (N, N′) and the loop figure (λ, μ) — avoid μ for elite count given the demand-mean μ clash — and rewrite the circular multi-echelon clamp q^w_t = min(q^w_t, …) (line 1559) with a raw-order symbol q̃^w_t.
8. **Number-formatting consistency.** (Writing W11, Figures V4, V7.) Use $\approx$ (not bare $\sim$) before numerals with a thin space (lines 3378/3380); apply {,} thousands separators uniformly to all 4+ digit costs (Table 16's 85395/91890.25/44170.26 lack them; Tables 12–13 have them); fix per-column decimal precision; in Table 5 (lines 1409–1412) append % to the "Learned vs. CDI" row or drop it from the A3C row and unify decimals.
9. **Orphan floats — add \ref callouts.** (Figures V2.) Seven tables are never cross-referenced, including the main-body Table 13 (the −36.8% divergent-K-T beat, defined line 2501) and the six appendix validation tables. Add an explicit "Table~\ref{…}" pointer for each.
10. **Table count (29) and Figure 6 caption.** (Figures V3, V6 [nits].) Consolidate the eight small per-problem parameter tables into one multi-panel appendix table (Table 4/tab:ds-params has two data rows); demote the all-ties Table 17 (+0.00% rows) and the companion Table 10 to appendix/merged rows; target ~18–20 main-body tables. Reword the Fig 6 caption (and body line 357's "side by side"): the figure draws only the one-hidden-layer net, so describe the linear backbone as "obtained by removing the hidden layer" or draw it as a second panel.

## 7. Per-dimension notes

- **Writing, clarity & structure:** Structurally excellent (parallel ten-problem scaffolding, central notation, self-contained captions, no jargon leakage), but severely over-long and over-hedged at the sentence level for ~71 pages. The dominant fix is *consolidation of repeated caveats* (item 6.3), which would also address the over-defensive tone. No writing issue is fatal, but the bloat would draw complaints at both OR and ML venues.
- **Novelty & positioning:** The conceptual core (learned decoder/action geometry) is real and well-articulated, and comparator characterization is a model of fairness. The vulnerability is the "incremental/engineering" critique, fed by the two uncited pillars (M5) and a thin three-paragraph related-work section missing the beer-game and differentiable-simulation lines (M7). Fixing the citations converts this from "asserted novelty" to "demonstrated." Several positioning claims lean on arXiv preprints (xie2024vc framed as "provably generalize," line 3776) — hedge or find published anchors (nit).
- **Technical & mathematical soundness:** The math itself is sound — the meta-reviewer independently re-derived the MMPP autocorrelation (0.356), every parameter count, the worked-example arithmetic, the echelon/installation conversion, and essentially every results-table percentage, all checking out. Remaining items are *protocol-consistency* (M1, M2, M3) and cosmetic figure/equation and notation mismatches (items 5.3, 5.4, 6.7), none undermining a central numerical claim.
- **Experimental rigor, statistics & reproducibility:** A serious, mostly commendable effort (explicit five-seed standard, CRN paired evaluation, disjoint seed blocks, validation appendix) that is *not applied uniformly* — the lost-sales surface (M1), best-of-N cost cells (M4), the PADN audit gap (M2), and the missing compute table (M6) are the concrete failures. The most-contested rows ARE properly seed-robust, so the fixes are bounded.
- **Claims-vs-evidence honesty:** Genuinely disciplined — losses are not buried, sub-band beats are downgraded to matches, cross-protocol rows are labeled. The residual concerns are framing choices that invite a more favorable reading than the evidence supports (the +450%/+524% ameliorating headline over a comparator capturing ~1% of the LP bound, lines 3366–3370; "previously-unsolved"; "match or beat published DRL" when the only clean DRL clear is A3C-via-proxy and the method loses to PPO), all disclosed in-section but not in the headline (item 5.12). One genuine internal inconsistency (M3) and one std convention slip (item 5.8).
- **OR / inventory domain correctness & validation:** Unusually disciplined; the validation appendix is genuinely executed, not snapshotted. Real fidelity items: silent demand recalibration of two cited processes (item 5.10), the asserted-not-validated dual-sourcing l_r=3,4 proxy (item 5.2), the catalog-literal vanilla optimum (item 5.11), and symbol/descriptor slips (items 5.3, 5.11). (i) and (ii) touch the faithfulness of two headline comparisons and should be flagged prominently.
- **Figures, tables & presentation:** On balance a strength — legible figures, professional TikZ diagrams, best-practice headline tables. Main defects: single-seed Tables 2–3 (= M1), seven orphan floats (item 6.9), 29 tables (consolidate), and scattered formatting inconsistencies (item 6.8). All bounded.

## 8. Top 5 priorities

1. **Fix the lost-sales reporting (M1).** Re-run the 72 cells over ≥5 optimizer seeds and re-derive 22/24 and 47/48 with dispersion — or explicitly exempt and soften the claim. This is contribution #1 and the single highest-impact fix; flagged by four reviewers.
2. **Correct the abstract's "robust" PADN claim and run the pending serial/assembly audit (M2),** so the headline matches the paper's own Table 17 footnote. Until audited, scope "robust" to the mixed topology only and propagate the caveat to the contributions and discussion.
3. **Resolve the 6-vs-5 multi-echelon seed contradiction (M3) and replace best-of-N cost cells with five-seed means (M4),** including adding optimizer-seed dispersion to the dual-sourcing cost cells and not bolding two single-seed runs as parallel results.
4. **Add the two missing load-bearing citations (M5: ES-tunes-heuristics anchors + soft-tree origins) and the beer-game / differentiable-simulation related work (M7),** converting asserted novelty into demonstrated novelty and closing the most conspicuous attribution gaps.
5. **Add a compute/wall-clock table and fill missing generation counts (M6),** so the central "more sample-efficient than DRL" claim is measured rather than asserted — then aggressively copyedit: split the abstract run-on, shrink contribution bullet 1, and consolidate the repeated caveats into Section 3 (items 6.1–6.3) to cut the bloat.
